phase_1.audio: Acoustic analysis reveals baseline of 75.3 dBA with periodic spikes up to 105.3 dBA throughout the 487s recording. One or more speech segments identified (avg confidence 0.86), suggesting social environment. Very loud environment detected; concentration significantly compromised.

phase_1.gps_motion: Geolocation data exhibits high initial uncertainty (2.9m) that improves to 1.4m as session progresses. Movement remains variable with user changing posture multiple times during session.

phase_2.correlations: Correlation analysis reveals moderate compatibility between positional, kinetic, and acoustic factors.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session exemplifies near-optimal conditions: minimal acoustic disturbance (82.3 dB), variable positioning, and strong focus indicators. Duration (487s) sufficient for sustained concentration assessment.

phase_3.recommendations: Invest in noise-canceling technology to mitigate high ambient levels. Establish dedicated seating to minimize postural shifts and maintain focus.

reason: Concentration score reflects moderate audio conditions (82.3dB), frequent motion, and adequate session length.
